title: Bridge Essay: Advances in understanding neural mechanisms of social dominance.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Wind/Pride** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Wind and pride — ego, narcissism, social dominance, self-esteem, status, social hierarchy in neuroscience, self-enhancement, hubris.

**Advances in understanding neural mechanisms of social dominance.** — Curr Opin Neurobiol. PMID: 29428628. DOI: 10.1016/j.conb.2018.01.006. Dominance hierarchy profoundly impacts social animals' survival, physical and mental health and reproductive success. As the measurements of dominance hierarchy in rodents become established, it is now possible to understand the neural mechanism mediating the intrinsic and extrinsic factors determining social hierarchy. This review summarizes the latest advances in assay development for measuring dominance hierarchy in laboratory mice. It also reviews our current understandings on how activity a...

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 4 (Wind/Pride). Evaluation: Excellent (score=10.8)
